title: toisusu urbaniana var. schneideri
intro: Toisusu urbaniana var. schneideri, also known as 'False Cinnamon' or 'False Cinnamon Tree', is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family. It is native to tropical Africa and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, treat digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
